title: Will The End of Evangelion ever be re released in the US
display_title: Will The End of Evangelion ever be re released in the US
wikitext:
[]: Will The End of Evangelion ever be re-released in the US?             Quote Post by GamlinKizaki » Sat May 14, 2011 5:28 pm Since the crummy Manga release of End of Eva went out of print 5-6 years back, is there any possibility that companies such as Funimation will eventually re-license End of Eva and re-release it properly? I was luckily able to find a copy of dvd for a decent price (that doesn't work on my bluray player, but on my old Ps2 I wanna add) a while back but I am appalled by how terrible the dvd quality is and thus have to resort to a copied DVD+R version of Renewal+ edited ZX subs I based off on the translated script (yes I know how bad of me,don't sue me) in order to properly watch it on my HD TV. What do you guys think, is End of Evangelion long due for a re-release on DVD or even Bluray?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat May 14, 2011 6:01 pm I really hate to say this, but if you can't find anything, FREAKIN EBAY IT! Or something. Look up on Amazon. And if all else fails... we'll I hope you can figure it out on you're own.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 14, 2011 6:06 pm Last I heard Funimation had approached Gainax about it. However I imagine the cost is pretty high, and wouldn't justify the licensing cost when it was pushed really hard for a number of years recently. I don't think it's going to happen in the immediate future. The best you can do is write to Funimation, and ask them to get it. They're probably the only entity with the funds to do it. Among other things, they need to know the demand is there. I would really love a proper DVD of it, personally.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sat May 14, 2011 6:16 pm I expect an EoE licensing announcement to come out sometime after a Japanese Blu-Ray release is done. Maybe in 2015.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Sat May 14, 2011 6:20 pm I think that it really should be a proper, new HD transfer to Blu-ray now. Think about it: if it's released on DVD (presumably sourced from same video that Renewal was) it'll quench the demand for a proper HD release just so many years more into future. Of course, it'd be against money-milking strategy to actually start off with the best stuff. Not to mention retransfer onto digital from film is a costly process they might not be wanting to do. And the studios in Japan might not be all that keen to touch the "old" evangelion now, either, that Rebuild is making the rounds. Still, one can dream...

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat May 14, 2011 6:27 pm It's all speculation at this point -- we've had many discussions on the topic before. But I would guess that the Japanese rights holders are focused on the new movies right now, so we won't see anything happen for a few years at the least.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat May 14, 2011 11:27 pm IIRC, didn't Manga almost bankrupt itself by buying the licensing rights to EoE? regardless, yeah, unless some media giant is willing to put up major licensing Yen, EoE, like many older anime titles, will not get a proper "HD" release, especially due to the costs of cleaning it up for 1080p video and DTS-HD audio there have been some BIG HD releases like GitS "2.0", but that is one of very few exceptions to the rule unfortunately

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun May 15, 2011 12:32 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: there have been some BIG HD releases like GitS "2.0", but that is one of very few exceptions to the rule unfortunately

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun May 15, 2011 4:47 am Going rate for EoE on ebay UK is 5-10GBP Either for the Manga retail version or the 'Manga Force' magazine free version which has all the same features as the retail one. I sold one of those a week or two ago for 7GBP.

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Sun May 15, 2011 8:18 am ^Your avatar contains too much win... Ontopic: Perhaps if some other EVA movies were released in America? Then they'd have a good reason to release their "prequel" too.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun May 15, 2011 1:10 pm There are also several versions of the movie on YouTube alone (one Japanese, the rest dubbed I think) with varying degrees of picture quality. I think veoh has it too although I'm not sure if that one is dubbed or subbed. Bottom line: I would like a DVD release but there are plenty of ways to watch it as long as you have an internet connection.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun May 15, 2011 1:26 pm I only know of one (maybe two) complete versions of EoE on Youtube, but that should be used only for reviewing scenes after you've already seen the movie - EoE is an experience that DEMANDS the largest screen available. Really though, a re-release (or rather, a release of the Renewal edition) sure would be nice. It saddens me that kids these days may be able to see the original series and not have any legal means to obtain the movie. Unfortunately, as others have pointed out, since Gainax is likely demanding an exorbitant sum in licensing fees, the Rebuild movies are the hot thing right now, and EoE doesn't exactly function as a standalone experience, odds are slim of seeing it too soon, at least until the original series and movies finally get their long-overdue Blu-Ray release in Japan. It'd be nice if Sentai Filmworks (a.k.a. Neo-ADV, i.e. the rights holders for the TV series) could license the movie and thus finally release a boxset containing the COMPLETE complete series, but given their money troubles and the fact that Gainax is in bed with Funimation now it's hard to see that happening - and as I've mentioned before, I imagine Matt Greenfield would be willing to literally kill a person before forfeiting the rights to Eva TV.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 15, 2011 1:30 pm If you're going to go to illegitimate methods to view The End of Evangelion, I suggest tracking down the Zhentarim Divx fansub: http://www.evamonkey.com/the-end-of-evangelion-fansub-by-zhentarim-divx/

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun May 15, 2011 1:43 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: http://www.evamonkey.com/the-end-of-evangelion-fansub-by-zhentarim-divx/

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun May 15, 2011 1:44 pm Aaron, quick question for those who do want to use that website... How do you get the file to work? When I click on it, all it gives me is info, nothing to click on.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 15, 2011 1:55 pm That's because we are not your personal

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 15, 2011 1:59 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Out of sheer curiosity, are there any major differences in translation between this fansub and the official subs? Or are they the same thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun May 15, 2011 3:05 pm http://cgi.ebay.co.uk/Manga-Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-End-Evangelion-DVD-/180665545164?pt=UK_CDsDVDs_DVDs_DVDs_GL&hash=item2a108171cc Copy of End of Eva on ebay UK - ending in 2 days.

[]:             Quote Post by Miuzaki » Sun May 15, 2011 5:34 pm I am just a bit far from willing to walk into funimation with an assault rifle and asking to see the director about a new release of EOE. (ok maybie not) BUT IT NEEDS TO HAPPEN!

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sun May 15, 2011 5:38 pm If anyone gets the rights it'll be Funi. IMO if rebuild stirs enough interest in Evangelion then they will pursue the rights for EOE after the final installment, but then again I did hear that it would cost a couple million to get the rights.
